approved_quote: this is not a disentanglement from, but a progressive knotting into
why_this_quote_helps: Syntactic inversion mirrors the preterite condition — escape negated; entrapment affirmed.
public_note: Elect/preterite theology at the novel's threshold.

approved_quote: Brennschluss. We don't have one. Or else it's classified.
why_this_quote_helps: The Allied intelligence gap as linguistic gap — technical vocabulary as institutional power.
public_note: Corporate-military knowledge regime in nine words.

approved_quote: It travels faster than the speed of sound. The first news you get of it is the blast.
why_this_quote_helps: The V-2's epistemological inversion: death precedes its own announcement.
public_note: The rocket's core physics — effect before cause.

approved_quote: a peculiar sensitivity to what is revealed in the sky. (But a hardon?)
why_this_quote_helps: Names the body/rocket correlation — the novel's founding anomaly that launches the entire pursuit.
public_note: The datum that starts everything.

approved_quote: He has become obsessed with the idea of a rocket with his name written on it
why_this_quote_helps: Pursuit and paranoia in one sentence — the personalisation of mass death.
public_note: The sentence the entire P01 plotline unpacks.

approved_quote: in infinite series just perceptibly, term by term, dying . . . but never quite to the zero
why_this_quote_helps: Mathematical entropy as familial dissolution — the asymptotic limit the novel's title inverts.
public_note: Science-control mode as the Slothrop genealogy's thermodynamics.

approved_quote: His time away, with Katje and Gottfried, has become shorter and more precious as the tempo of firings quickens.
why_this_quote_helps: Intimacy compressed by Rocket firing rhythm — the triad's temporal condition.
public_note: A compact marker for the Blicero triad.

approved_quote: the strayed children, the wood-wife in the edible house, the captivity, the fattening, the Oven
why_this_quote_helps: Litany naming the Maerchen template that structures the entire Blicero/Katje/Gottfried triangle.
public_note: Fairy-tale as sacrificial script.

approved_quote: Are they Elect, or are they Preterite, and doomed as dodoes?
why_this_quote_helps: The elect-preterite theological question applied directly to the act of extermination — colonisers and dodos in the same soteriological framework.
public_note: Genocide as theology — the dodo subplot's hinge.

approved_quote: They are in love. Fuck the war.
why_this_quote_helps: The romance counterpoint's sharpest hinge: private attachment against wartime machinery.
public_note: Roger/Jessica emotional counterpoint.

approved_quote: a Puritan reflex of seeking other orders behind the visible, also known as paranoia
why_this_quote_helps: The novel's most concise definition of its own epistemology — triggered as Slothrop senses the apparatus.
public_note: Names the novel's governing epistemic mode.

approved_quote: perversely he waits for a sudden noise to begin his day, a first rocket
why_this_quote_helps: Conditioned London reflex persisting on the Riviera — the stimulus-response pattern has followed him south.
public_note: The body's conditioning survives the move to the Zone.

approved_quote: nothing more—or less—sinister than a new plastic, an aromatic heterocyclic polymer
why_this_quote_helps: Turns the system-revelation anchor into material evidence: the Rocket plot is also chemistry and corporate file.
public_note: Names the Imipolex discovery as chemistry.

approved_quote: Signs will find him here in the Zone, and ancestors will reassert themselves.
why_this_quote_helps: Names the Zone as a field of signs and recurrence — transition into Part III.
public_note: Marks Zone entry as a shift into signs and inheritance.

approved_quote: skidded out onto the Zone like a planchette on a Ouija board
why_this_quote_helps: Zone arrival as occult automatism — Slothrop is moved through rather than entering.
public_note: Pursuit becoming passive medium.

approved_quote: Forget frontiers now. Forget subdivisions. There aren't any.
why_this_quote_helps: Three sentences of decreasing length enacting the erasure they describe — the Zone's dissolution of sovereignty.
public_note: Dissolved borders enabling Part 3's picaresque.

approved_quote: leaving in their crippled military rage pieces, modules, airframe sections, batteries rotting
why_this_quote_helps: Rocket infrastructure decomposing into readable ruin — the corporate body's death as pursuit-object.
public_note: Infrastructure as debris trail.

approved_quote: In and out of all the vibrant flesh moves the mad scavenger Tchitcherine
why_this_quote_helps: Gives Tchitcherine a kinetic anchor inside the eastern movement of the novel.
public_note: Anchors Tchitcherine in motion through the sequence.

approved_quote: Pökler was an extension of the Rocket, long before it was ever built.
why_this_quote_helps: Explains why the Pokler episode sits so heavily in the score: family history bound to Rocket design.
public_note: Family and Rocket as one continuous line.

approved_quote: the gift of Daedalus that allowed him to put as much labyrinth as required between himself and the inconveniences of caring
why_this_quote_helps: Engineering skill as moral distance — professional competence as the architecture of avoidance.
public_note: Technical complicity as labyrinth-building.

approved_quote: love something like the persistence of vision, for They have used it to create for him the moving image of a daughter
why_this_quote_helps: The daughter as cinematic illusion — one frame per summer; love as the projector mechanism.
public_note: Film/body mode literalised through family loss.

approved_quote: All his vacuums, his labyrinths, had been the other side of this
why_this_quote_helps: Engineering vocabulary revealed as always structurally adjacent to atrocity — oblique and devastating.
public_note: The moment of recognition at Dora.

approved_quote: Somewhere, among the wastes of the World, is the key that will bring us back, restore us to our Earth and to our freedom.
why_this_quote_helps: The counter-text as messianic search — recovery of territory and ontological standing.
public_note: The Schwarzkommando search-thesis distilled.

approved_quote: I know what my voice sounds like—heard it at Peenemünde years ago on Weissmann's Dictaphone
why_this_quote_helps: Colonial history returning through the coloniser's apparatus — Enzian's voice captured at the rocket facility.
public_note: The colonial record inside the rocket programme.

approved_quote: there'll have to be something done about Ombindi, Empty Ones, doctrine of the Final Zero
why_this_quote_helps: Names the factional divide directly — 00001 counter-text vs Final Zero self-extinction.
public_note: The internal Schwarzkommando debate.

approved_quote: a Corporate City-state where technology was the source of power, the engineer worked closely with the administrator
why_this_quote_helps: Metropolis as the fantasy-template that Peenemünde and Mittelwerke will realise.
public_note: Film as blueprint for technocratic complicity.

approved_quote: move beyond life, toward the inorganic. Here is no frailty, no mortality—here is Strength, and the Timeless
why_this_quote_helps: Jamf converts chemistry into transcendence-rhetoric — the ideological seed of the entire technical programme.
public_note: Chemistry as ideology.

approved_quote: How could he tell her that the dramatic connections were really all there, in his dreams? How could he tell her anything?
why_this_quote_helps: The marriage fractures because the engineer's inner life is already cinematic — domestic speech fails.
public_note: The first irreversible stage of family loss.

approved_quote: Of course it happened. Of course it didn't happen.
why_this_quote_helps: Epistemological impossibility — sacrifice as simultaneously fact and unintegratable event.
public_note: The novel's central paradox in nine words.

approved_quote: the sudden, solid arrival of loss, loss forever, the irreversible end of love, of hope
why_this_quote_helps: Irreversibility as physical sensation — loss given mass and weight.
public_note: The sacrificial break as embodied event.

approved_quote: Whatever went on on those eyeballs when you weren't looking would just be lost. You'd only have fragments
why_this_quote_helps: Meta-textual: the central event recoverable only as shards — a statement about the novel's own epistemic condition.
public_note: The witness can retain only fragments.

approved_quote: He only wants now to be out of the winter, inside the Oven's warmth, darkness, steel shelter
why_this_quote_helps: Blicero's death-drive inverts the fairy-tale threat — the Oven becomes consummation. Echoes back to 1.14's fairy-tale template.
public_note: Cross-episode echo: 1.14 Maerchen fulfilled in 4.05 launch.

approved_quote: This is only one of many American Mysteries
why_this_quote_helps: Marks the late Part IV scatter as a chain of American fragments.
public_note: Frames the late scatter as one among many unresolved fragments.

approved_quote: The two, boy and Rocket, concurrently designed.
why_this_quote_helps: Compact terminal anchor binding human body and Rocket design in one sentence.
public_note: Body and Rocket as concurrent design.

approved_quote: The screen is a dim page spread before us, white and silent. The film has broken, or a projector bulb has burned out.
why_this_quote_helps: Screen becomes page — cinema collapses into text at the novel's close; the reader becomes the final audience.
public_note: The theatre as reading space.

approved_quote: absolutely and forever without sound, reaches its last unmeasurable gap above the roof of this old theatre, the last delta-t
why_this_quote_helps: The parabola frozen — infinite proximity without contact; annihilation suspended as permanent present tense.
public_note: The rocket suspended above the audience.

anchor_label: Touch the person next to you
approved_quote: There is time, if you need the comfort, to touch the person next to you, or to reach between your own cold legs
why_this_quote_helps: Narrator addresses the audience directly — reader and theatre-goer become one preterite congregation awaiting descent.
public_note: The final direct address to the reader.
